channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: An office known for enforcing special education is now focused on Trump's political priorities
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department's Office for Civil Rights is known best for enforcing the right to disability services across America’s schools. But under President Donald Trump, it’s taking a frontline role in his political battles.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Credit card debt calculator: Find out how much you owe
item.description: Americans have more credit card debt than ever. Figure out how much you owe and how long it will take to pay back.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: OPM inspector general investigating DOGE emails, access to agency data
item.description: The inspector general for the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) told lawmakers he is investigating the use of a new data system at the agency that has been part of a broader effort by Elon Musk and his allies to email federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: One of Earth’s most ferocious predators may have looked very different than we thought
item.description: Few prehistoric monsters capture the imagination quite like the megalodon. From natural history museums to the silver screen, this colossal shark, which went extinct over three million years ago, has been depicted as one of the most terrifying and ferocious predators to ever roam the planet.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: X hit by 'massive cyberattack,' Musk says amid intermittent outages Monday
item.description: Elon Musk says there was a "massive cyberattack against X" on Monday amid several outages to the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Menendez brothers: Los Angeles DA Nathan Hochman asks to withdraw predecessor's motion to free killers
item.description: Los Angeles District Attorney Nathan Hochman is seeking to withdraw his predecessor's motion to seek reduced sentences for the Menendez brothers, serving life.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arizona prisoner set to be executed next week passes up chance to ask for reprieve
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A prisoner scheduled to be executed next week in what would be Arizona’s first use of the death penalty in over two years has passed up a chance to ask for a reprieve from his death sentence.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scholars stranded in America and abroad amid funding freeze of State Department programs
item.description: Fulbright grant recipient Aubrey Lay was supposed to get paid for three months of work by the U.S. government through his teaching assistantship at a school for Ukrainian refugees in Estonia. Instead, he only got about one week's pay and no word on when he might see the rest of his grant.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Convicted Pentagon leaker Jack Teixeira faces court-martial while already serving 15-year sentence
item.description: Massachusetts Air National Guard member Jack Teixeira, convicted of leaking Pentagon documents on the Ukraine war, faces court-martial Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stocks plunge as Wall Street wonders how much economic pain Trump will accept
item.description: The economy has already given some signals of weakening, mostly through surveys showing increased pessimism.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, KRON4

item.title: Trump isn't alone in his geopolitical aspirations. Some in Illinois and Oregon want change too
item.description: As President Donald Trump floats grand ideas like reacquiring the Panama Canal and taking over Greenland, some rural residents of Illinois and Oregon are promoting geopolitical change of their own: They want to break away from their states, and perhaps unite with Indiana and Idaho.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire risk remains high on Long Island after brush fires
item.description: The fire danger remains high on Long Island as officials work to quell wind-driven brush fires that broke out Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KRON4

item.title: See it: Activists vandalize Trump's Scotland golf resort
item.description: Pro-Palestinian activists said Saturday they vandalized one of Trump's golf resorts in Scotland in response to his proposal to empty the Gaza Strip of its Palestinian population.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: An early version of the Egg McMuffin included a surprising condiment
item.description: For a brief time, McDonald's envisioned their now-signature breakfast sandwich with a sweet component.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Israel will send a delegation to Qatar to try to 'advance' ceasefire negotiations
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel said that it would send a delegation to Qatar on Monday “in an effort to advance the negotiations” around the ceasefire in Gaza, while Hamas reported “positive signals” in talks with Egyptian and Qatari mediators on starting negotiations on the truce's delayed
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 2 days of clashes and revenge killings in Syria leave more than 1,000 people dead
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The death toll from two days of clashes between Syrian security forces and loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad and revenge killings that followed has risen to more than 1,000, a war monitoring group said Saturday, making it one of the deadliest acts of violence since Syria’s con
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy released after Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Xavier Worthy was released Saturday after a district attorney in Texas declined to pursue charges against the Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver following an arrest in which he had been accused of felony domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas bill could charge transgender people with 'gender identity fraud', if passed
item.description: A bill filed in the Texas House of Representatives this week threatens to charge transgender Texans with a crime for identifying with a gender that differs from their sex assigned at birth.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, KTLA 5

item.title: Pickleball isn't named after a dog: How the sport really earned its moniker
item.description: You may have heard a rumor about the game being named after a dog. Spoiler alert: That isn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohio couple charged after hundreds of living and dead rats found inside home
item.description: A Stark County couple is facing animal cruelty charges after authorities discovered horrific conditions inside their home, which included hundreds of living and dead rats.
item.dc:creator: Jack Shea, Fox 5

item.title: Judge awards nearly $2M after concluding FBI agent was negligent in shooting kidnapped Texas man
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge has awarded nearly $2 million in damages as part of a civil lawsuit after concluding an FBI agent was negligent when he fatally shot a kidnapped Texas man during a botched rescue attempt in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hawks, butterflies and a song for her murdered son help Georgia mom reclaim joy after killings
item.description: ROSWELL, Ga. (AP) — A hawk swooped in and landed on a tree, then watched as Michele and Jeremy Davis mourned their murdered son at a vigil outside his high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A total lunar eclipse and blood moon will be visible in March: How and when to watch
item.description: A treat awaits sky gazers in the month of March – the moon will take on a deep, reddish hue as it slips into the Earth's shadow during a total lunar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Watch the moon turn red during a total lunar eclipse in March
item.description: A total lunar eclipse will flush the moon red Thursday night into Friday morning across the Western Hemisphere.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN AP Science Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Unions ask court to stop DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of labor unions are asking a federal court for an emergency order to stop Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing the sensitive Social Security data of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Storm dumps heavy snow on northern Arizona, gridlocking Interstate 40
item.description: A winter storm dumped heavy snow across northern Arizona on Friday, playing a major factor in a more than 15-mile (24-kilometer) backup on a major interstate.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Takeaways from the Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa deaths investigation
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A towering figure in cinema for decades, retired actor Gene Hackman spent his final years in seclusion with his wife, living with heart disease and Alzheimer's, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Expert tells judge to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams without letting them be refiled
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge has no choice but to grant the Justice Department’s unusual and divisive request to dismiss New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case, a court-appointed lawyer said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Late car payments hit highest level in decades
item.description: Americans are falling behind on their monthly car payments at the highest rate in more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, ABC 4

item.title: Raiders acquire quarterback Geno Smith from Seahawks for a 3rd-round pick, AP source says
item.description: The Las Vegas Raiders answered their most pressing personnel question of the offseason Friday by acquiring quarterback Geno Smith in a trade with Seattle and reuniting him with his former coach, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A car pulled from a river may tell what happened to an Oregon family of 5 that went missing in 1958
item.description: After two days of dredging, a crane on Friday pulled from the Columbia River a Ford station wagon that officials believe belonged to an Oregon family who disappeared while on a trip 66 years ago to find Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A timeline of how actor Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa died at their New Mexico home
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Actor Gene Hackman died of heart disease a full week after his wife died from hantavirus in their New Mexico home, likely unaware that she was dead because he was in the advanced stages of Alzheimer’s disease, authorities revealed Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Moon mission goes sideways: Private lunar lander declared dead after botched landing
item.description: A private lunar lander is no longer working after landing sideways in a crater near the moon’s south pole and its mission is over, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: By MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC
